info.title: Mobile CLI Server API
info.description: JSON-RPC API for mobile device automation and control

methods.name: devices.list
methods.description: Returns a list of all connected mobile devices (iOS and Android)

params.name: includeOffline
params.description: Include offline devices in the list

params.name: platform
params.description: Filter devices by platform (ios or android)

params.name: type
params.description: Filter devices by type (device or simulator)

methods.name: device.screenshot
methods.summary: Take a screenshot of a device
methods.description: Captures a screenshot from the specified device and returns it as base64 data

params.name: quality
params.description: Image quality (1-100, only used for JPEG)

params.name: clip
params.description: Optional rectangle to crop the screenshot to, in screen coordinates

methods.name: device.screencapture
methods.description: Starts screen capture streaming for the specified device. Supports MJPEG (iOS and Android) and AVC/H.264 (Android only) formats.

params.name: format
params.description: Video format - 'mjpeg' for MJPEG stream (iOS and Android) or 'avc' for H.264 stream (Android only)

params.name: quality
params.description: Video quality (only used for MJPEG format)

result.name: stream
result.description: Video stream - multipart/x-mixed-replace for MJPEG or video/h264 for AVC

methods.name: device.io.tap
methods.description: Performs a tap gesture at the specified coordinates on the device screen

methods.name: device.io.longpress
methods.description: Performs a long press gesture at the specified coordinates on the device screen

params.name: x
params.description: X coordinate for the long press

params.name: y
params.description: Y coordinate for the long press

params.name: duration
params.description: Duration of the long press in milliseconds

methods.name: device.io.text
methods.description: Inputs the specified text on the device

methods.name: device.io.button
methods.description: Presses a physical or virtual button on the device

methods.name: device.io.gesture
methods.description: Performs a custom gesture with multiple actions on the device

params.name: actions
params.description: List of gesture actions to perform

methods.name: device.url
methods.description: Opens the specified URL on the device

methods.name: device.io.swipe
methods.description: Performs a swipe gesture from one coordinate to another on the device screen

methods.name: device.info
methods.description: Returns detailed information about the specified device

methods.name: device.io.orientation.get
methods.description: Returns the current orientation of the device screen

methods.name: device.io.orientation.set
methods.description: Sets the orientation of the device screen

methods.name: device.boot
methods.description: Boots the specified device (simulators/emulators only)

methods.name: device.shutdown
methods.description: Shuts down the specified device (simulators/emulators only)

methods.name: device.reboot
methods.description: Reboots the specified device (simulators/emulators only)

methods.name: device.dump.ui
methods.description: Dumps the UI hierarchy of the device screen

methods.name: device.apps.launch
methods.description: Launches an application by bundle ID on the specified device

params.name: bundleId
params.description: Bundle ID of the application to launch

params.name: locales
params.description: BCP 47 locale tags to set for the app (e.g. ["fr-FR", "en-GB"]). On iOS this is a per-launch argument. On Android 13+ this is persistent.

methods.name: device.apps.terminate
methods.description: Terminates a running application by bundle ID on the specified device

params.name: bundleId
params.description: Bundle ID of the application to terminate

terminated.description: True if the app was running and got terminated, false if it was not running

methods.name: device.apps.list
methods.description: Returns a list of installed applications on the specified device

methods.name: device.apps.foreground
methods.description: Returns the currently foreground (active) application on the specified device

methods.name: device.apps.install
methods.description: Installs an application on the specified device from a local file path. Supports optional IPA re-signing for real iOS devices.

params.name: path
params.description: Local file path to the application package (.apk, .ipa, or .app)

params.name: forceResign
params.description: Re-sign the IPA with a local provisioning profile before installing (only for .ipa files on real iOS devices)

params.name: provisioningProfile
params.description: Path to a .mobileprovision file to use for re-signing. If not provided, a matching profile is auto-detected.

params.name: signingIdentity
params.description: Signing identity name or SHA-1 hash to use for re-signing. If not provided, a matching identity is auto-detected.

methods.name: device.apps.uninstall
methods.description: Uninstalls an application from the specified device by its bundle/package ID

params.name: bundleId
params.description: Bundle identifier (iOS) or package name (Android) of the application to uninstall

methods.name: device.apps.clear
methods.description: Clears all data (cache, preferences, databases) for an application without uninstalling it. Supported on Android and iOS Simulator. Not supported on real iOS devices.

params.name: bundleId
params.description: Bundle identifier (iOS) or package name (Android) of the application to clear

methods.name: device.apps.path
methods.description: Returns the on-device path to an app's data container. Currently supported on Android and iOS real devices.

params.name: bundleId
params.description: Bundle identifier (iOS) or package name (Android) of the application

path.description: Absolute path to the app's data container on the device

methods.name: device.fs.ls
methods.description: Lists files and directories at a given path on the device, or in an app's container. Defaults to the device root if no path is given. Supported on Android and iOS Simulator.

params.name: bundleId
params.description: Bundle identifier to list files inside an app's container

params.name: remotePath
params.description: Absolute path on the device to list. Defaults to device root if omitted.

size.description: File size in bytes (0 for directories)

isDir.description: True if this entry is a directory

methods.name: device.fs.pull
methods.summary: Pull a file from the device
methods.description: Downloads a file from the device and returns its contents as base64. Maximum file size is 1 MB. Supports arbitrary paths on Android and iOS Simulator.

params.name: remotePath
params.description: Absolute path of the file on the device

methods.name: device.fs.push
methods.summary: Push a file to the device
methods.description: Uploads a file to the device from base64-encoded content. Maximum file size is 1 MB. For /data/user/ paths on Android, the file is staged via /data/local/tmp and copied with run-as.

params.name: remotePath
params.description: Absolute destination path on the device

params.name: content
params.description: Base64-encoded file contents to write. Maximum decoded size is 1 MB.

methods.name: device.fs.mkdir
methods.summary: Create a directory on the device
methods.description: Creates a directory at the specified path on the device or in an app's container.

params.name: bundleId
params.description: Bundle identifier to create directory inside an app's container

params.name: remotePath
params.description: Absolute path of the directory to create

params.name: parents
params.description: Create parent directories as needed (like mkdir -p)

methods.name: device.fs.rm
methods.summary: Remove a file or directory on the device
methods.description: Removes a file or directory at the specified path on the device or in an app's container.

params.name: bundleId
params.description: Bundle identifier to remove files inside an app's container

params.name: remotePath
params.description: Absolute path of the file or directory to remove

params.name: recursive
params.description: Remove directories and their contents recursively (like rm -rf)

methods.name: device.crashes.list
methods.description: Returns a list of crash reports from the specified device. Supports iOS real devices (via crashreport service), iOS simulators (reads from DiagnosticReports), and Android devices (parses adb logcat crash buffer).

processName.description: Name of the process that crashed

id.description: Unique identifier for the crash report, used with device.crashes.get

methods.name: device.crashes.get
methods.description: Returns the full content of a specific crash report by ID. The ID is obtained from device.crashes.list.

methods.name: device.webview.list
methods.description: Returns all embedded webviews currently visible in the foreground app on the device. Browser apps (Safari, Chrome) are not included.

methods.name: device.webview.content
methods.description: Returns the full outer HTML of the page currently loaded in the attached webview.

result.name: content
result.description: Full outer HTML of the page

methods.name: device.webview.goto
methods.description: Navigates the attached webview to the given URL and optionally waits for a load state.

params.name: waitUntil
params.description: Load state to wait for after navigation

methods.name: device.webview.reload
methods.description: Reloads the current page in the attached webview.

params.name: waitUntil
params.description: Load state to wait for after reload

methods.name: device.webview.goBack
methods.description: Navigates one step back in the attached webview's session history.

methods.name: device.webview.goForward
methods.description: Navigates one step forward in the attached webview's session history.

methods.name: device.webview.url
methods.description: Returns the current top-level URL (location.href) of the attached webview.

methods.name: device.webview.title
methods.description: Returns the document title (document.title) of the page currently loaded in the attached webview.

methods.name: device.webview.query
methods.description: Finds elements matching a CSS selector and returns their tag, text, id, class, value, and href.

params.name: selector
params.description: CSS selector to match elements against

text.description: Trimmed text content, truncated to 200 characters

methods.name: device.webview.evaluate
methods.description: Evaluates a JavaScript expression in the attached webview and returns the JSON-serialized result. Throws WebViewEvaluateError if the script throws.

params.name: expression
params.description: JavaScript expression to evaluate. May be a function body invoked with args.

params.name: args
params.description: Arguments passed to the expression when it is a function body

methods.name: device.webview.waitForLoadState
methods.description: Polls the attached webview server-side until the requested load state is reached or the timeout elapses.

methods.name: server.info
methods.description: Returns the server name and version

methods.name: server.shutdown
methods.description: Initiates a graceful server shutdown

ParseError.data: Invalid JSON was received by the server

InvalidRequest.data: The JSON sent is not a valid Request object

MethodNotFound.data: The method does not exist or is not available

DeviceNotFound.data: The specified device does not exist

DeviceTimeout.data: The device did not respond in time

WebViewNotFound.data: The supplied webview id does not match any currently-attached webview

WebViewSessionExpired.data: The session is no longer valid; the client must re-attach to the webview

WebViewNodeNotFound.data: The supplied node id is stale because the DOM has mutated; the client must re-query

WebViewNavigationFailed.data: The webview did not reach the requested load state in time

WebViewEvaluateError.data: The script passed to device.webview.evaluate threw an exception

DeviceProvider.description: Describes where the device is provided from

sessionId.description: Session identifier for this device allocation

data.description: Base64 encoded image data with data URI prefix

WebView.description: An embedded webview attached to the foreground app

id.description: Stable handle for this webview within the device session

bundleId.description: Bundle ID (iOS) or package name (Android) of the host app

bounds.description: Webview position on screen, in screen coordinates

isVisible.description: True when the webview has non-zero on-screen bounds
